title: Shri Satyanarayan Vrat Katha
intro: Read Shri Satyanarayan Vrat Katha in English and receive the divine blessings of Lord Vishnu for peace, prosperity, and success.
description:
Shri Satyanarayan Vrat Katha: Chapter 1

Suta Ji said: In the Naimisharanya pilgrimage site, 88,000 sages, led by Shaunaka, asked Shri Suta Ji, "O Lord! How can people in this Kali Yuga, who are devoid of Vedic knowledge, attain devotion to God? And how will they be liberated? O great among sages! Please tell us of a penance by which one can attain merit in a short time and fulfill all desired wishes."

"We wish to hear such a story." Suta Ji, the knower of all scriptures, replied: "O you who are worshiped among the Vaishnavas! You have asked for the welfare of all beings. Therefore, I will tell you of an excellent vow that Sage Narada asked of Lakshmi-Narayana, and which the Lord of Lakshmi told to the great sage Narada. Listen carefully."

Once, the Yogi Narada, desiring the welfare of others, traveled through many worlds and arrived at the mortal world (earth). Here, he saw almost all humans, born in various forms of life, suffering from numerous miseries due to their own actions. Seeing their suffering, Narada thought, "What can be done so that the miseries of mankind can be put to an end?" Thinking on this, he went to Vishnu-Loka (the abode of Vishnu). There, he began to praise the Lord of the Gods, Narayana, who held the conch, discus, mace, and lotus in his hands and wore a garland around his neck.

While praising him, Narada said, "O Lord! You are endowed with supreme power; neither mind nor speech can attain you. You have no beginning, middle, or end. You are the formless cause of creation, the remover of devotees' sorrows. My salutations to you." Hearing Narada's praise, Lord Vishnu said, "O great sage! What is on your mind? For what purpose have you come? Speak without hesitation."

Narada replied, "O Lord, humans born in many forms of life on earth are suffering greatly due to their deeds. O Lord! If you have compassion for me, please tell me how those humans can be liberated from their miseries with minimal effort."

Shri Hari said, "O Narada! You have asked a very good question for the welfare of humanity. I will tell you of the act that frees humans from delusion; listen. There is a rare and excellent vow in both the celestial world and the mortal world that bestows merit. Today, out of love, I tell it to you. A human who performs this Vrat of Shri Satyanarayan with proper rituals experiences happiness here and attains liberation after death."

Hearing the words of Shri Hari, Narada asked, "What is the fruit of this vow? What is its procedure? Who performed this vow? And on which day should this be done? Please tell me everything in detail." Hearing Narada, Shri Hari said, "This vow removes sorrow and distress and leads to victory in all places. Humans should perform the worship of Shri Satyanarayan in the evening with devotion and faith, in the company of Brahmins and relatives. With devotion, take an offering (Naivedya) consisting of bananas, ghee, milk, and wheat flour (in one-and-a-quarter proportion). Use 'Sathi' flour, sugar, and jaggery instead of wheat flour, and offer this mixture as Bhog to the Lord."

"Feed the Brahmins and relatives, and then eat yourself. Immerse yourself in the devotion of the Lord with hymns and Kirtans. Performing this Vrat of Shri Satyanarayan in this manner surely fulfills all the desires of a human. This is the only simple path to liberation in this Kali Yuga on earth."

॥ The first chapter of Shri Satyanarayan Vrat Katha is complete. Say, Glory to Lord Shri Satyanarayan. ॥

Shri Satyanarayan Vrat Katha: Chapter 2

Suta Ji said, "O Sages! Now I will tell you the history of the one who performed this vow in earlier times; listen carefully. In the beautiful city of Kashi, there lived an extremely poor Brahmin. Distressed by hunger and thirst, he used to wander the earth."

"The Lord, who loves Brahmins, took the form of an old Brahmin one day and went to him, asking, 'O learned one! Why do you wander the earth suffering daily?' The poor Brahmin replied, 'I am a poor Brahmin. I wander the earth for alms. O Lord! If you know any remedy for this, please tell me.' The old Brahmin replied that Lord Satyanarayan is the granter of all desired wishes, so you should worship Him. By doing this, a human is freed from all miseries."

"Lord Satyanarayan, having come as the old Brahmin, explained the entire procedure of the Vrat to the poor Brahmin and then disappeared. The Brahmin began to think that he would certainly perform the Vrat that the old Brahmin had spoken of. After deciding this, he could not sleep that night. He woke up early the next morning, resolved to perform the Vrat, and went out for alms. That day, the poor Brahmin received a great deal of wealth in alms. With that wealth, he joined with his relatives and successfully performed the Vrat of Shri Satyanarayan. After performing the Vrat, the poor Brahmin was freed from all his miseries and became blessed with various kinds of wealth."

"From that time on, this Brahmin began performing this Vrat every month. In this way, whoever performs the Vrat of Lord Satyanarayan will be freed from all sins and will attain liberation. Any person who performs this Vrat will also be freed from all miseries."

"Suta Ji said, 'Thus, I have told you the Vrat of Shri Satyanarayan as told by Narayana to Narada. O Brahmins! What more shall I say?' The sages asked, 'O great sage! Who else heard from that Brahmin and performed this Vrat in the world? We wish to hear this, as our minds are filled with devotion for it.'"

"Suta Ji replied, 'O Sages! Listen to all those who have performed this Vrat! Once, that same Brahmin, according to his wealth and prosperity, prepared to perform this Vrat with his relatives. At that time, an old woodcutter came, left his wood outside, and went inside the Brahmin's house. Distressed by thirst, seeing them performing the Vrat, he bowed to the Brahmin and asked, "What are you doing?" The Brahmin told him he was performing a Vrat and explained what fruit it would bring, asking him to listen as well. The Brahmin said that this Vrat of Lord Satyanarayan fulfills all desires. It is by His grace that wealth and prosperity have increased in my house.'"

"Learning about the Satyanarayan Vrat from the Brahmin, the woodcutter was very pleased. After receiving the Charanamrit and eating the Prasad, he went home. The woodcutter resolved in his mind that whatever money he earned from selling wood today, he would use to perform the excellent Vrat of Shri Satyanarayan. With this thought in mind, the old man carried the wood on his head and went to sell it in a city where wealthy people lived. In that city, he received four times the usual price for his wood."

"Overjoyed, the old man took the money and bought bananas, sugar, ghee, milk, curd, wheat flour, and other materials for the Satyanarayan Vrat and went home. There, he called his relatives and performed the worship and Vrat of Shri Satyanarayan according to the prescribed rituals. By the effect of this Vrat, the old woodcutter became blessed with wealth, children, and all worldly comforts, and at the end of his life, he went to Vaikuntha Dham."

॥ The second chapter of Shri Satyanarayan Vrat Katha is complete. Say, Glory to Lord Shri Satyanarayan. ॥

Shri Satyanarayan Vrat Katha: Chapter 3

Suta Ji said, "O great sages, now I will tell you the further story. In ancient times, there was an intelligent King named Ulkamukh. He was a speaker of truth and self-controlled. He used to visit holy places daily and alleviate the suffering of the poor by giving them wealth. His wife was as beautiful as a lotus and was a chaste woman. On the banks of the Bhadrashila River, they performed the Vrat of Shri Satyanarayan. At that time, a merchant named Sadhu arrived. He had a lot of wealth for trade. Seeing the King performing the Vrat, he asked with humility, 'O King! Filled with devotion, what are you doing? I wish to hear, so please tell me.'"

"The King said, 'O Sadhu! Along with my relatives, I am performing the vow and worship of the mighty Shri Satyanarayan for the attainment of progeny.' Hearing the King's words, Sadhu asked with respect, 'O King! Please tell me the entire procedure of this Vrat. According to your instructions, I will also perform this Vrat. I do not have children, and by performing this, I will surely be blessed with offspring.' Hearing the entire procedure from the King, he went home after finishing his trade."

"Sadhu the merchant described this Vrat, which grants progeny, to his wife and said that when he had a child, he would perform this Vrat. Sadhu said this to his wife Leelavati. One day, by the grace of Lord Satyanarayan, Leelavati became pregnant. In the tenth month, she gave birth to a beautiful daughter. She began to grow day by day like the moon of the waxing phase. Her parents named the girl Kalavati."

"One day, Leelavati reminded her husband in sweet words that the time to perform the Vrat he had pledged to Lord Satyanarayan had arrived, and asked him to perform it. Sadhu said, 'O dear! I will perform this Vrat at the time of her marriage.' After assuring his wife, he went to the city. Kalavati grew up in her father's house."

"One day, when Sadhu saw his daughter with her friends in the city, he immediately called a messenger and asked him to find a suitable groom for his daughter. Hearing this, the messenger went to the city of Kanchana, looked around, and brought a suitable merchant's son. Seeing the suitable groom, Sadhu called his relatives and married off his daughter, but unfortunately, Sadhu still did not perform the Vrat of Shri Satyanarayan."

"Upon this, the Lord became angry and cursed the merchant to face severe suffering. Skilled in his work, Sadhu the merchant went to the city of Ratnasarpur, located by the sea, with his son-in-law. There, both the father-in-law and son-in-law began trading in the city of King Chandraketu. One day, by the Maya of Lord Satyanarayan, a thief was fleeing after stealing the King's wealth. Seeing the King's soldiers chasing him, he hid the stolen wealth where Sadhu was staying with his son-in-law. The King's soldiers saw the King's wealth near Sadhu the merchant, so they tied them both up and happily took them to the King, saying, 'We have caught these two thieves; please give orders for further action.'"

"By the King's order, both were thrown into a difficult prison, and all their wealth was confiscated. Due to the curse of Shri Satyanarayan, Sadhu's wife also suffered greatly. Thieves stole the wealth that was kept at home. Suffering from physical and mental pain and hunger and thirst, Kalavati went to a Brahmin's house in search of food. There, she saw the Vrat of Shri Satyanarayan being performed, heard the story, accepted the Prasad, and returned home at night. Her mother asked Kalavati, 'O daughter, where were you until now? What is on your mind?'"

"Kalavati told her mother, 'O mother! I have seen the Vrat of Shri Satyanarayan at a Brahmin's house.' Hearing the daughter's words, Leelavati began preparing for the worship of the Lord. Leelavati performed the worship of Satyanarayan with her family and relatives and prayed for her husband and son-in-law to return home soon. She also prayed that He would forgive all their faults."

"Shri Satyanarayan was satisfied with this Vrat and appeared to King Chandraketu in a dream, saying: 'O King! Release those two merchants and return the wealth you have taken from them. If you do not do this, I will destroy your wealth, kingdom, and progeny.' Having said this to the King, He disappeared."

"In the morning, in the assembly, the King narrated his dream and then said, 'Release the merchant's sons from prison and bring them to the assembly.' As soon as the two arrived, they bowed to the King. The King spoke in a sweet voice, 'O great men! By fate, you have received such difficult suffering, but now you have no fear.' Saying this, the King also gave them new clothes and ornaments and returned double the wealth that had been taken from them. Both merchants then started their journey home."

॥ The third chapter of Shri Satyanarayan Vrat Katha is complete. Say, Glory to Lord Shri Satyanarayan. ॥

Shri Satyanarayan Vrat Katha: Chapter 4

Suta Ji said, "The merchant performed the auspicious rites and began his journey towards his city. After traveling a short distance, a monk-disguised Shri Satyanarayan asked him, 'O Sadhu, what is in your boat?' The arrogant merchant laughed and replied, 'O Monk! Why do you ask? Do you wish to take my wealth? My boat is filled with leaves and twigs.' Hearing the merchant's harsh words, the Lord said, 'May your words become true!' The monk left after saying this and sat on the seashore at a distance. After the monk left, Sadhu the merchant, after his daily routine, saw the boat rising high. Upon seeing leaves and twigs in the boat instead of wealth, he fainted and fell to the ground."

"When he regained consciousness, he was drowned in deep sorrow. Then his son-in-law said, 'Do not despair; this is the monk's curse. Therefore, we must seek His refuge; only then will our desires be fulfilled.' Hearing his son-in-law, he went to the monk, bowed with great devotion, and said, 'Please forgive me for the false words I spoke to you.' Saying this, he wept bitterly. Then the Lord in the form of the monk said, 'O Merchant's son! You have received suffering repeatedly due to my command. You turned away from my worship.' Sadhu replied, 'O Lord! Even the gods like Brahma do not know your form, so how could an ignorant person like me know it? Please be pleased; now I will worship you according to my capacity. Protect me and fill the boat with wealth as it was before.'"

"Hearing the merchant's devotional words, the Lord was pleased, granted him the boon he desired, and disappeared. When the father-in-law and son-in-law returned to the boat, it was filled with wealth. They then worshipped Lord Satyanarayan there with their companions and set out for their city. When they reached near the city, they sent a messenger to inform the family. The messenger bowed to the merchant's wife and said, 'Master has arrived near the city with your son-in-law.'"

"Hearing the messenger, Sadhu's wife Leelavati performed the worship of Lord Satyanarayan with great joy and said to her daughter Kalavati, 'I am going to see my husband; you finish the work and come soon!' Hearing her mother's words, Kalavati hurriedly left the Prasad behind and went to her husband. Due to the neglect of the Prasad, Lord Satyanarayan became angry and drowned her husband along with the boat in the water. Not finding her husband there, Kalavati fell to the ground weeping."

"Seeing the boat drowned and the girl weeping, the merchant was distressed and said, 'O Lord! Please forgive the mistake made by me and my family.' Hearing the merchant's humble words, Lord Satyanarayan was pleased, and a voice from the sky was heard: 'O Merchant! Your daughter has come leaving my Prasad behind, which is why her husband has disappeared. If she goes home, eats the Prasad, and returns, she will surely find her husband.' Hearing this heavenly voice, Kalavati reached home, ate the Prasad, and then returned to see her husband. After that, the merchant worshipped Lord Satyanarayan with his relatives according to the rituals. After enjoying happiness in this world, he goes to heaven in the end."

॥ The fourth chapter of Shri Satyanarayan Vrat Katha is complete. Say, Glory to Lord Shri Satyanarayan. ॥

Shri Satyanarayan Vrat Katha: Chapter 5

Suta Ji said, "O Sages! I will narrate one more story; listen carefully! There was a King named Tungadhwaja who was absorbed in ruling his subjects. He also suffered greatly by rejecting the Lord's Prasad. Once, while in the forest hunting wild animals, he came under a Banyan tree. There, he saw cowherds worshipping Lord Satyanarayan with great devotion along with their relatives. Due to his arrogance, the King did not go to the place of worship despite seeing them, nor did he bow to the Lord. The cowherds offered the King the Prasad, but he did not eat it; instead, he left the Prasad there and went to his city."

"When he reached the city, he found everything devastated. He immediately understood that all this was done by the Lord. He went back to the cowherds, performed the worship according to the rituals, and ate the Prasad. By the grace of Lord Satyanarayan, everything became as it was before. After enjoying happiness for a long time, he attained the heavenly realm after death."

"Whoever performs this extremely rare Vrat will attain wealth and grain by the compassion of Lord Satyanarayan. The poor become wealthy and live a life free from fear. The childless attain the joy of having children, and upon the fulfillment of all desires, the human attains Vaikuntha Dham at the end of their life."

"Suta Ji said, 'Now I will tell you the story of the next birth of those who performed this Vrat earlier. The old Brahmin was reborn as Sudama and attained liberation. The woodcutter was reborn as Nishad in his next birth and attained liberation. The King named Ulkamukh was reborn as Dasharath and went to Vaikuntha. The merchant named Sadhu was reborn as Moradhwaja and attained liberation after sacrificing his son. King Tungadhwaja was reborn as Swayambhu and attained liberation by performing deeds with devotion to the Lord.'"

॥ The fifth chapter of Shri Satyanarayan Vrat Katha is complete. Say, Glory to Lord Shri Satyanarayan. ॥

faq.q1: When should the Shri Satyanarayan Vrat be performed?
faq.a1: The Shri Satyanarayan Vrat can be performed on any auspicious occasion, Purnima (Full Moon), Sankranti, or Fridays. It is traditionally considered best to perform this ritual in the evening.
faq.q2: Can anyone perform the Shri Satyanarayan Vrat?
faq.a2: Yes, anyone—man or woman, married or unmarried—can perform this Vrat with full devotion and faith. There are no restrictions on who can observe it.
faq.q3: Can Satyanarayan Katha be performed at home?
faq.a3: Yes, this Vrat and Katha are specifically meant to be performed at home with family. You can either invite a priest (Pandit Ji) to conduct it or perform it yourself following the proper rituals.
faq.q4: What should be included in the Satyanarayan Prasad?
faq.a4: The Prasad typically consists of bananas, sugar or jaggery, milk, ghee, and wheat flour. These are mixed to create the 'Prasad' and 'Charanamrit'. Adding a Tulsi leaf to the Prasad is mandatory.
faq.q5: What are the benefits of observing the Shri Satyanarayan Vrat?
faq.a5: Observing this Vrat brings peace and prosperity to the home, increases wealth and happiness, alleviates physical and mental suffering, and helps in the fulfillment of heartfelt wishes.
